query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Intricacy', 'The state or quality of being intricate or entangled', 'The essay highlights the intricacy of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intricate', 'Very detailed and complicated', '“The artist’s intricate designs took months to complete.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intrigue', 'To conceive or carry out a secret plan intended to harm', 'The author hoped the new policy would intrigue tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intrinsic', 'Belonging naturally; essential', '“The intrinsic value of gold makes it a desirable asset.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Introductory', 'Introducing, giving a preview or idea of', 'Her introductory became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Introgression', 'Genes of one species introduced in the gene pool of another', 'Her introgression became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intromit', 'To send, put or let in, to introduce, to admit', 'The author hoped the new policy would intromit tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Introspect', 'Consider one''s own internal state or feelings', 'Scholars noted a striking introspect between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Introvert', 'One who focuses on their own mind, feelings, or affairs', 'The essay highlights the introvert of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intrude', 'To enter without permission', 'Critics argued that the plan might intrude the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intuition', 'Immediate cognition without the use of rational processes', 'Scholars noted a striking intuition between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intuitive', 'Using or based on what one feels to be true', 'The reviewer described the tone as intuitive, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inundate', 'To cover with large amounts of water, to flood', 'The author hoped the new policy would inundate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inundation', 'An overflow, a flood', 'Her inundation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inur', 'To accustom to hardship, difficulty, pain, etc', 'Critics argued that the plan might inur the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inure', 'To be accustomed to something, particularly something unpleasant', 'Over time, the community learned to inure its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Invalid', 'An infirm or sickly person', 'Scholars noted a striking invalid between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Invalidate', 'To make invalid, especially applied to contract law', 'Over time, the community learned to invalidate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Invaluable', 'Extremely useful; indispensable', 'The reviewer described the tone as invaluable, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Invariable', 'Not variable, constant', 'What seemed invariable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Invasion', 'A military action of armed forces entering another territory', 'Her invasion became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Invective', 'Insulting or abusive language', 'Scholars noted a striking invective between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inveigh', 'To protest strongly or attack vehemently with words', 'Critics argued that the plan might inveigh the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inveigle', 'To convert, convince or win over with flattery or wiles', 'The author hoped the new policy would inveigle tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inventive', 'Creative and original', '“The scientist was known for his inventive problem-solving skills.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inverse', 'Reversed position, direction, or tendency', 'Scholars noted a striking inverse between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inversion', 'The action of inverting', 'The essay highlights the inversion of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Invert', 'To turn (something) upside down or inside out', 'Critics argued that the plan might invert the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Investigator', 'One who investigates', 'Her investigator became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Investor', 'A person who invests money in order to make a profit', 'Critics argued that the plan might investor the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inveterate', 'Having a particular habit long established and unlikely to change', 'Critics argued that the plan might inveterate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Invidious', 'Calculated to create ill will or resentment or give offense', 'The author hoped the new policy would invidious tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Invigorate', 'To impart vigor, strength, or vitality to', 'The author hoped the new policy would invigorate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Invincible', 'Impossible to defeat, destroy or kill', 'Over time, the community learned to invincible its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inviolable', 'Not susceptible of violence or of being profaned/corrupted', 'The passage presents a inviolable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Invocation', 'The act of calling for the presence of some superior being', 'The essay highlights the invocation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Invoke', 'To call upon or refer to something', '“The speech invokes themes of justice and equality.”', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Involuntarily', 'Without conscious control', '“She involuntarily gasped at the shocking news.”', 'adverb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Involuntary', 'Without intention, unintentional', 'Scholars noted a striking involuntary between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Involution', 'Entanglement, a spiralling inwards, intricacy', 'Scholars noted a striking involution between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Involve', 'To roll or fold up, to wind round, to entwine', 'Over time, the community learned to involve its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Invulnerable', 'Incapable of being wounded, or of receiving injury', 'What seemed invulnerable at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inwardly', 'In or on, or with reference to, the inside or inner part', 'The results were inwardly linked to changes in public policy.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Iota', 'The ninth, and smallest, letter of the greek alphabet', 'Scholars noted a striking iota between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irascible', 'Easily angered', 'What seemed irascible at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irate', 'Extremely angry, wrathful, enraged', 'The reviewer described the tone as irate, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ire', 'To anger, to fret, to irritate', 'The author hoped the new policy would ire tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Iridescent', 'Producing a display of lustrous, rainbow-like colors', 'The reviewer described the tone as iridescent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irk', 'To irritate, annoy, bother', 'The author hoped the new policy would irk tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Irksome', 'Disagreeable or troublesome, bothersome, annoying', 'Her irksome became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
